videos.transcript: What's up guys, we're here in Phuket. It's one of the most popular islands to visit in Thailand and in this video we're going to show you the best things to do here. So hope you enjoy it! Phuket is located on the Andaman Sea in southern Thailand. It's the biggest island in the country and thanks to its easy access via an international airport as well as a bridge to the mainland, it's also one of the most popular tourist hot spots. Most people are attracted to its sandy beaches, vibrant nightlife, and delicious Thai cuisine. But there's so much more to discover than that. In this video, we highlight 10 of the best things to do on Phuket Island, including some exciting day trips. Make sure to watch until the end because we also share three travel tips so you won't make the same mistakes that we did. Now let's dive in! We're Zac and Ine. We make helpful videos about destinations around the world so you can easily plan your own adventures. With over 30 beaches to choose from, Phuket has something for everyone. If you like the more popular party beaches with lots of activities, then Patong or Karon Beach might be for you. And if you enjoy quieter, more serene beaches then look no further than Yan Nui or Freedom Beach. Our favorite one called Nai Harn Beach had the best of both worlds. This beach doesn't attract large crowds yet, but does offer some activities and facilities. Keep in mind that the beaches in Phuket are best visited between November and May. At that time you won't be fighting rough waves that can make swimming and snorkeling dangerous. Wherever you go in Thailand, the streets come alive in the evenings with night markets filled with delicious street food options and souvenir stalls. The most popular and authentic one on the island is the Sunday Walking Street Market in Phuket Town. But if you're not visiting on a Sunday, you can browse for other night markets in the area. Besides Phuket Town, you'll also find them at Patong, Karon, and Bangtao Beach. The Indy Market in Phuket Town is also an amazing spot to enjoy local dishes. All of the markets on the island provide a great opportunity to sample Thai cuisine. As the capital of the Phuket Province, Phuket town has a rich history which is reflected in the architecture of its Old Town today. Strolling around Thalang Road, you'll find colorful Chinese-European style houses. There are several souvenir and handicraft shops as well as some cafes and restaurants along the way. The street isn't very long and you don't need more than an hour or two to walk around here. One of the things we enjoyed most about Phuket was its proximity to nearby highlights, such as the stunning Phang Nga Bay. This natural beauty is shaped by towering limestone cliffs, emerald waters, and numerous islands to explore. From Phuket, it's possible to go on a one day tour here. The first stop on the tour is generally the Monkey Cave Temple, where you can find brilliant Buddha images and of course lots of monkeys. Panyee Island is another popular stop on the route and a place we really enjoyed. We got here by longtail boat and were able to explore the amazing village built on stilts by local fishermen. We also had some delicious Thai food on the island. Talu Cave was the next stop on our tour, where we explored its beautiful blue waters and rock formations by canoe. And finally, we visited the famous James Bond Island which got its name from the 1974 James Bond movie "The Man with the Golden Gun". You can find a link to the tour that we joined in the description below the video. If you're going to go into the water here and take pictures at James Bond Island you should definitely be a little bit careful because there's things like monitor lizards, stingrays, and broken seashells. Patong is known as the party capital of Phuket, if not all of Thailand. Bangla Road is the notorious heart of all Phuket nightlife with a variety of live music bars and nightclubs to choose from. The road turns into a pedestrian walking street after 6 pm and you can expect large crowds later in the evening. One of the most iconic landmarks on the island is undoubtedly the Big Buddha. This 45 meter or 147 foot tall white statue is at the top of a hill and offers spectacular views of the island. The best way to get here is by rented vehicle, taxi, or tuk-tuk. It's also possible to hike here from Kata Beach, but keep in mind that it's a steep, long, and sweaty route. Besides the Big Buddha, there are tons of other breathtaking viewpoints. One of the most popular areas to take in the scenery is Karon Viewpoint, which is accessible by road and gives you a lovely view of three beaches. But our favorite view was Black Rock Viewpoint in the south of Phuket. It's the most challenging view to reach as it involves first driving a scooter or car over a sketchy dirt road as well as some steep walking. If you hike the trail from the last parking lot on the dirt road like we did, it takes about 15 minutes to get to the top. There's also an option to do a longer trail and we've put a link to both trail options in the description if you want to follow it on your phone. There are plenty of places to watch the sunset in Phuket, whether you head to one of the beaches on the west coast such as Patong or go to a higher lookout point. Prom Thep Cape is likely the most visited sunset spot on Phuket Island. But for a more relaxing experience, try to secure a spot at one of the bars on the west-facing hill with a panoramic view, just a three minute drive from Karon viewpoint. After Beach Bar and Restaurant is a good one. You cannot leave Thailand without visiting at least one of its Buddhist temples. Wat Chalong is the largest and arguably most beautiful one in Phuket. Dating back to the 1800s is also an important place of worship for the Thai people. Make sure to visit the Grand Pagoda, which protects a fragment of Lord Buddha's bone. You can book a half day tour to this temple, the Big Buddha and Phuket Old Town via the link in the description below the video. But there are also more than 25 other temples in Phuket, so you might find one closer to you as well. When you visit these Buddhist temples in Phuket, make sure to really dress appropriately to respect the culture, which means like you have to cover your knees as well as your shoulders, and also like sunglasses and caps should be taken off. Finally, there are numerous beautiful islands that are only a short boat right away from Phuket. The most popular ones to visit on a day tour are undeniably the Phi Phi Islands, which includes the beautiful Maya Bay. Here, you can find white sandy beaches, crystal clear waters, and spectacular natural scenery. You might recognize the idyllic setting of Maya Bay from the movie "The Beach" with Leonardo DiCaprio. We included a link to the Phi Phi Islands Day Tour from Phuket in the description below as well. But if you're looking for less crowded Islands to visit it might be best to look at some other options, such as Koh Yao, the Similan Islands, Coral Island, and Racha Island. So pick your favorite! As we promised at the beginning of our video, we'll now share three things that we learned in Phuket that will help your own trip. First, the best area to stay is the southwest side of the island. This is because most attractions and beaches are located there. We made the mistake of staying in Phuket Town, which was a far drive for most of the highlights. There's also a mountain range on the island that runs north to south, which means you have to drive over a steep mountain pass to get from east to west or vice versa. Secondly, the traffic is unforgiving all around Phuket and you can expect large traffic jams, especially on the West Coast. Many tourists decide to rent a scooter to avoid it, but you have to be very cautious because we saw many accidents. Make sure to follow all the traffic rules as well if you do rent a vehicle. Chances are very high that you'll get pulled over by the police at some point and you'll have to pay a fine if any rules are broken at all. We learned this the hard way. Finally, as you can probably understand from the previous two tips, don't expect a peaceful paradise island from Phuket. The island is heavily loaded with tourists in all of the popular areas. There are a lot of fun things to do here, but keep in mind that it's always busy. If you're looking for a laid-back, tranquil Island, then we recommend looking into the smaller islands that are off the shore of Phuket or in the Gulf of Thailand. You can check out the other Thailand videos on our channel for more island destination options if tranquility is more your vibe. And of course there are a few things to do in Phuket that we didn't mention and we've included a list of those on the screen here for you. If you're planning your Thailand trip, we recommend keeping things organized with our interactive travel planner. Find a discount code below. We also included important links that you'll need for your Phuket trip in the description, such as a full travel guide with where to stay and eat. We'll catch you on the next adventure!

videos.title: Best Things To Do in Phuket Thailand 4K
videos.description:
Are you looking for the best things to do in Phuket? We just created a list of 40 must do activities for you to do while visiting Phuket Thailand! In this Phuket travel guide we show you around Patong Beach, Kata Beach, Phuket Town, Phi Phi Islands and many other islands and beaches across the island.  Some of those Phuket attractions include Bangla Road, James Bond Island, Naka Weekend Market, Big Buddha, Muay Thai, Thai Cooking class, Andamanda waterpark, Phuket Bird Park, Elephants and much more!  If you enjoy this Phuket travel video you can also watch our other travel vlogs from across Thailand.

It took me 7 days to make this Phuket vlog. I would say you can explore the beaches around Patong and Bangla in 2 days but 3 days is ideal. You should factor in what islands you want to see keeping in mind that each island hopping tour is basically a full day tour. For sure Phi Phi islands tour and Hong Island are full day adventures. You will leave early in the morning and come back in the late afternoon.

Whether heading to Bangkok, Phuket, Chiang Mai, Koh Samui, Koh Phangan or any other place in Thailand for that matter, these travel tips will help you prepare for your upcoming trip. From transportation options to avoiding scams, these are travel tips I don't hear often people speak about as often but are in my opinion essential for a great Thailand visit.

0:28 - Download Line

3:13 - Cash is king here
3:28 - Break down your bills

cheap and very easy to set up you just gotta make sure that your phone is unlocked and you're good to go for this trip I got a one month plan with unlimited 4G for less than 30 bucks and you can set contacts a lot of local businesses are active on this app as well so it's great if you want to order some products or make a reservation of some sort to give an example my favorite massage spot in videos.transcript: There is no place in Thailand that receives as many negative reviews as the island of Phuket in recent years the richest and most expensive province has gone from being a crowd favorite to being almost a no go area what happened why do hundreds of thousands of people still go there and how can you make the most of a trip if you want to save time and money then this video is for you the ultimate Phuket guide with a map you'll learn how to get there and how to get around where you can lose money and where you can lose fat there are also tips on food hotels and nightlife plus recommendations for excursions and at the end you'll get the best travel plan for a few days in Phuket chapter 1 what's Phuket and what's wrong with it Phuket is a province in the Andaman sea it consists of 33 islands Phuket is connected by the sarasin bridge to Phang Nga Province to the north the next nearest province is Krabi at 222 square miles Phuket is about the size of Ibiza the Isle of Man and Guam so far so good what's wrong well Phuket is a classic example of over-tourism there are around 118 Travelers for every resident in addition around a quarter of the residents are foreigners this automatically leads to a rapid loss of Local Charm while tourists used to be annoyed by scams like overpriced Tuk Tuks today they are also annoyed by overcrowding and the rudeness of certain people chapter 2 how to get there around 70 Airlines fly to the island's airport with direct international flights from Frankfurt Oslo Copenhagen and Istanbul for example here is an overview of all connections but there are a lot more options by flight from Bangkok both from the international airport and from the mainly domestic airport either directly to Phuket or to other airports nearby and then continue by ferry or bus or by train and then by bus if you prefer to drive for hours you can of course do that too chapter three how to get around assuming you flew here what happens next you could rent a car at the airport or you take a taxi grab limousine shared mini bus or you can take the cheap Smart Bus but that takes a while and it only goes to the West Coast if you just have to go to the capital you can also take the airport bus once you check in the real traffic problems begin depending on your location if you live in Patong or the capital you can do many things on foot or with the grab app and motorcycle taxis however if you want to travel longer distances you are taking a risk to protect your money and your health Tuk Tuks are absolutely not recommended just Google Tuk Tuk Mafia Phuket tip use the comfortable Smart Bus and otherwise book a tour links in the video description chapter 4 where is what you have already seen the airport Patong and the capital now comes the rest first the beaches those in the west are nicer. then the mountains there is the big Buddha and here a look at the beautiful surroundings chapter 5 which area is for whom the destination is divided into three districts 17 subdistricts and around 100 villages the area in the north covers about half of the island however this is the least touristy area only bangtao attracts many guests so if you want to be a little quieter and quicker to get to the airport you should check in here good for families and couples the small District in the middle is home to kamala Beach and Patong Beach it's wild and chaotic here good for single men and party tourists the South includes the West and East coasts great beaches the beautiful Capital Excursion peers and a few Islands ideal for everyone but far from the airport and the crazy nighlife chapter six who you will meet according to the Thai tourism Authority around 11 million tourists come to the island every year most Travelers are from these countries Russia China India Australia United Kingdom because Phuket is so touristy it seems to be the perfect introduction to the world of long travel for many so you meet a lot of naive budget tourists who see themselves as a gift to the Thai people chapter 7 when to come there are three seasons on the island dry super hot and rainy the postcard season lasts from December to March after which it is very hot followed by the Rainy phase from May to November so if you want to save money and prefer to have fewer tourists around come in the rainy season chapter 8 where to stay apparently there are around 120,000 hotel rooms on the island here are a few accommodation recommendations with pool in almost every corner the prices here are generally higher but sometimes cheaper depending on the season chapter 9 what to do there is so much to do here both on Phuket and in the surrounding areas let's start in the north there is an island with a floating football field below is the James Bond Rock the dog rescue station is north of the airport from this beach you can see the planes up close here are some elephant sanctuaries and here the kickbox stadiums the most beautiful Viewpoint is in the South further east the epicenter of the Island's Fitness industry is in rawai but especially in chalong this street in particular is legendary it's basically just gyms with attached accommodations here you can experience The Biggest Loser in real life recommended Titan Tiger unit 27 from here you can visit the big Buddha then we continue to the capital Portuguese architecture great markets a beautiful Viewpoint there is a floating Beach Club further north the Excursion boats leave from the East Coast just take a look at what you can visit in the Bay from Leonardo DiCaprio's Beach to chicken Island to Krabi links to the best tours in the video description chapter 10 where to eat according to the Michelin guide there is only one outstanding restaurant on Phuket and 25 very good ones most of them are in the capital and none of them in Patong Beach let's start in the north Michelin recommends four restaurants there including the best the rest of the West Coast is considered less tasty but that's not entirely true my tip in patong you can buy seafood at this market and have it prepared on the first floor there are two great restaurants in the South plus the fish market with attached kitchens in chalong this gem is located in a mangrove swamp now the restaurants in the capital there are some good ones in the area too here is an overview of the markets so it's safe to say there is enough to eat chapter chap 11 the nightlife in Thailand there are always the four big bees bar beer Billiards babes what else starts with b? write a comment but if you feel like going crazy then you're in good hands in the West beach towns aren't bad but the epicenter of nightlife is Patong and by nightlife I mean beer bars nightclubs go- go bars here are four hotpots for beer bars one of them is the Island's most famous address Bangla Road just look at the Variety if you want to dance you can go to these nightclubs and if you want to watch others dance go to this side street it wouldn't be wrong to call the street Suzy Wong. Pro tip stay away from drunk tourists ignore vendors and remain respectful chapter 12 how to spend one week in Phuket day one let's say you arrived here on a Saturday then take the airport bus to the capital for 100 Baht there check into the Casa blanca Hotel and then take a grab or motorbike taxi up to the hill Before Sunset then dinner in one of the oldest Food Shops afterwards live music day two breakfast in this 70-year-old restaurant then to the park or one of the many temples in the in the evening to the night market in the old town day three check out off to Patong with grab app check in at the hotel Clover off to the beach in the evening go to the seafood market and have your food cooked there then bungler Road day four in the morning take the Smart bus to one of the other beaches and eat there then back and in the evening watch a fight snacks at Bangla Night Market day five book a tour in advance hop from Island to Island all day long evening Bangla Road day six check out to Bangtao with grab app check in at the Best Western Hotel get a massage on the beach and eat there in the evening then to the Heavenly bar day seven check out and off to the airport if you have time take a closer look at the planes here if Thailand's biggest island isn't exciting enough for you try Bangkok or Pattaya here's everything you need to know and don't forget to like share comment subscribe

videos.description:
From Surin and Kamala Beach to Patong and Phuket Town, follow me across Thailand’s biggest island. You’ll see well-known spots like Bang Tao Beach and Promthep Cape, but also hidden bays, quiet beaches, and places most visitors miss.

I’ll share practical tips on when to visit, how to get around, and where different types of travelers might feel at home. Some parts of Phuket are crowded, others feel untouched. And that contrast is what makes the island worth exploring.

videos.transcript: Phuket is an island of contrasts. Quiet coes next to crowded streets, fishing villages not far from luxury resorts, temples standing above beaches that draw millions. But you can still find parts of Phuket without the big crowds. And I'm going to show you where to go depending on what you're looking for. Welcome to Thailand's largest island. Surin Beach was once lined with beach clubs, shops, and terraces. In recent years, much of that was removed, restoring its natural feel. Clean sand, clear water, and age-old sunsets that feel calmer than before. It's a rare note of quiet on this side of Puket. You'll still find vendors and loungers, but now the beach belongs more to the sea than the buildings. [Music] Kamala is a relaxed shoreline just south of Surin. The town behind the beach is midsized with a mix of local shops, hotels, and a night market that's known for good, affordable Thai food. It's calmer than Pong and better suited to families, couples, and travelers who want convenience without chaos. It hosts Phuket Fantasy for those curious about a cultural show. On the north end, Cafe Delmare brings an upscale beach club vibe featured in The White Lotus, though the series was actually filmed on Co Simui. Drinks and loungers here don't come cheap. But the sunsets are breathtaking. Walk far enough south and the beach turns quieter, almost like two places in one. I wasn't sure if I wanted to include this beach because it's one of my favorite places in Phuket and not well known. This tucked away bay lies between Kamala and Surin and access changes with the season. Most of the time I will walk there. It takes about 20 minutes along a stone path which can be tough if you're not in good shape. You can also arrive by boat or take a steep path down from the road. But once you're there, the reward is blue water, sunshine, and an almost perfect silence. >> There's only one small restaurant nearby, but its views are unforgettable. [Music] You're not [Music] I guess we have to talk about Patong. Pong is Fuket's most famous beach, known for nightife, shopping, and endless bars. For some travelers, this is the heart of Fuket tourism. I can't really recommend it. It's not my thing. For me, Bangala Road, the famous party street, is hell on earth. Although it can be an interesting experience because it's so crazy. But some people love the energy here. If you do want to visit, it's worth knowing that Pong has the widest range of hotels, restaurants, and entertainment on the island. So, it can work as a base for exploring other parts of Phuket. [Music] Proteep Cape is the southern tip of Phuket and one of the island's most photographed spots at sunset. You can get here easily by road about 25 minutes from Pong or 15 minutes from Roway. And the drive itself is worth it for the coastal views. Most people gather at the viewpoint by the lighthouse. But for something different, you can also hire a boat from Rawai Beach and watch the sun go down from the water. It's a simple idea, but it turns the sunset into an experience you'll never forget. The Big Buddha once stood tall above Fuket Town. Since the August 2024 landslide that killed 13 people and damaged homes below, the site remains closed indefinitely. Locals remind travelers that the views are better from nearby vantage points like Karon Viewpoint instead. It's a landmark that will likely return, but for now it stands as a reminder of how fragile the island can be. Located within Sirinat National Park, Nayang is one of Phuket's quieter shores. Pine and casuarina trees line the sand, and local families visit with blankets and baskets, not jet skis. The vibe is simple. hammocks, street food, and a beach that feels lived in rather than designed. It's an underrated beach that is also close to the airport. In the north part, it's popular to stand under the airplanes when they land or take off. Although, this is not the place if you want to party. Nihorn beach lies in the south of Phuket, not far from Pram Theep Cape. Unlike Pong or Kata, it has managed to stay more relaxed with just a few hotels and restaurants around the lagoon. The sand is soft and wide, and the water is calm during the dry season, making it a favorite for families and people who prefer a slower pace. The small village behind the beach has cafes and local shops, but it never feels overbuilt. If you're looking for one of Phuket's most balanced beaches where locals still come for a swim at the end of the day, Niharn is a good choice. Sometimes you just need to get away from everything. A shadowed trail up stone stairs behind Kamala leads to Cathu Waterfall, a series of drop pools best seen during the rainy months of June to November. It's not dramatic. It was pretty dry when I was there, but it's authentically tropical. It's a reminder that Fuket isn't just sea and sand, but also dense jungle and hidden streams. Just south of the airport, Banana Beach lies behind a short path and feels almost hidden. The facilities are sparse, but the waves are clear, the sand soft, and the arrival feels more like discovery than tourism. On weekends, locals gather here, too. Proof that not every good spot is only for travelers. It's also a good spot for snorkeling. [Music] Nui is a small tuckedin beach accessed by a rough inland path or boat. Once inside, you'll find a managed beach area with chairs and snacks, fewer crowds, and a reward for reaching it. Its exclusivity is part of the appeal. More effort, fewer people. [Music] Phuket town on the island's east side is often skipped by beach travelers, but it's one of the most interesting parts of Phuket. The old quarter is filled with colorful Cenino Portuguese shopouses, a reminder of the island's history as a tin mining hub during the 19th century. Walking streets like Thallang Road have cafes, murals, and small museums that give a very different feel than the West Coast. At night, the Sunday walking street market fills with food stalls and music. If you want to step away from the beaches and see the cultural side of the island, Phuket Town is worth at least an afternoon. [Music] >> Bangal beach is one of the longest beaches on Phuket. Stretching for about 6 km, it has a bit of everything. Luxury resorts, beach clubs, and quieter stretches where you can walk for a long time without seeing many people. It's a good choice if you want space, but also easy access to restaurants and nightife nearby. One of the most well-known spots here is the Lagona area, a complex of hotels, villas, and a golf course built around lagoons. You'll also find beach clubs like Tatch Beach Club, which give the area a more upscale feel. My tip is to go to the northern half of the beach. It's calm even in high season. The southern part is for beach clubs and hotels. [Music] Fuket has a tropical climate with two main seasons. The dry season runs from November to April with steady sunshine and calm seas. Although April is a very hot month. May to October is monsoon season when rain is frequent and waves are stronger, especially in October. though it can still be a good time if you don't mind some showers and want fewer crowds. Getting around is fairly easy. Taxis and ride hailing apps are available, but they can be expensive for longer trips. Renting a scooter is common, but it comes with risks. Thailand has some of the most dangerous roads in the world, and accidents involving tourists are frequent, so only consider it if you're very confident. Another option is to hire a driver for the day, which is safer and often more relaxing if you want to explore the island. Phuket might have a reputation for being very touristic, and sure, some places are, but it's also an island where you can still find tranquility and beautiful surroundings. It's big, and I can't cover everything in one video. I'll go back and maybe you'll see me there searching for new places and enjoying that famous Thai hospitality. It's not perfect, but after all, this is the land of smiles.
